summary.title: Post-Order Experience PRD Studio
summary.primary_metric: Post-order support contacts per 100 active orders

journey.buyer_need: Learn about schedule risk early enough to adjust downstream plans.
journey.primary_signal: Late delay disclosure creates avoidable tickets and buyer distrust.
journey.prd_opportunity: Launch proactive delay alerts with reason codes, revised ETA, confidence, and next action.

journey.buyer_need: Resolve an exception without repeating context across support, operations, and supplier teams.
journey.primary_signal: Support workflows lack a clear path from ticket intake to accountable resolution.
journey.prd_opportunity: Create an exception workspace that links order context, owner, SLA timer, and buyer-facing status copy.

journey.buyer_need: Understand whether parts are actively being produced and what changed since the last visit.
journey.primary_signal: Static status labels do not explain whether a custom job is progressing.
journey.prd_opportunity: Replace static status copy with line-item milestones, last update freshness, and next expected update.

journey.buyer_need: See that a matched supplier has accepted the work and understands key requirements.
journey.primary_signal: The handoff from marketplace order to manufacturing work is opaque.
journey.prd_opportunity: Expose a supplier kickoff milestone with requirement acknowledgement and owner timestamp.

journey.buyer_need: Track packages, partial shipments, packing documents, and expected delivery in one place.
journey.primary_signal: Shipment data exists, but line-item and package-level views are fragmented.
journey.prd_opportunity: Unify line-item shipment tracking, package status, downloadable packing lists, and delivery exceptions.

journey.buyer_need: Know the order was accepted, reviewed, and ready for supplier kickoff.
journey.primary_signal: Buyers ask whether the order is truly in motion after checkout.
journey.prd_opportunity: Add a confirmation panel that separates payment received, manufacturability review, and supplier assignment.

features.problem: Buyers learn about custom manufacturing delays too late to adjust their own production plans.
features.user_story: As a buyer, I want delay reasons, revised ETA, and next update timing so I can reset internal expectations.
features.dependency: Supplier reason-code capture and ETA confidence service
features.acceptance_criteria: Delay alert fires before SLA breach, includes reason code, revised ETA, confidence band, and next update owner.

features.problem: Roadmap decisions are weakened when support tickets lack consistent post-order reasons.
features.user_story: As a PM, I want support contacts tagged to journey moments so I can prioritize product fixes.
features.dependency: Support taxonomy, agent workflow changes, and QA sampling
features.acceptance_criteria: At least 90 percent of post-order tickets contain journey stage, reason code, and resolution path.

features.problem: A single order status hides what is happening across custom parts, partial shipments, and production steps.
features.user_story: As a procurement lead, I want each line item to show current milestone and next expected update.
features.dependency: Event normalization across supplier updates, QA checkpoints, and shipping events
features.acceptance_criteria: Each line item shows current milestone, last update age, next step, and exception state.
features.metric: Order status page return rate after support contact

features.problem: Buyers with many active orders cannot quickly see which orders changed since the prior visit.
features.user_story: As a program manager, I want a digest of changed orders so I can focus on risk instead of checking every order.
features.dependency: Notification preferences and account-level order grouping
features.acceptance_criteria: Digest summarizes changed milestones, delayed orders, shipped line items, and unresolved support items.

features.problem: Packing lists, tracking numbers, and partial shipment details are hard to inspect together.
features.user_story: As a buyer, I want shipping documents and tracking grouped by line item and package.
features.dependency: Carrier tracking links and document availability service
features.acceptance_criteria: Buyer can access package tracking, packing list, carrier, shipped items, and exception state in one view.

features.problem: Support, operations, and supplier teams need one record of what changed and who owns the next action.
features.user_story: As a support agent, I want order context and owner routing so I can resolve exceptions without manual investigation.
features.dependency: Support ticket integration and escalation taxonomy
features.acceptance_criteria: Every exception has owner, SLA timer, root cause, customer-safe update copy, and next action.

experiments.name: Early delay alert
experiments.hypothesis: Sending a delay alert with reason, revised ETA, and next update timing will reduce delay-related support contacts.
experiments.population: Orders with projected SLA risk above 50 and no shipment scan.
experiments.primary_metric: Delay support contacts per 100 affected orders
experiments.guardrail: No increase in cancellation rate or negative CSAT comments about alert quality.
experiments.ship_decision: Ship if contact rate drops by at least 12 percent and guardrails remain flat.

experiments.name: Line-item milestone clarity
experiments.hypothesis: Showing current milestone and next expected update will lower repeated order-status page visits after a ticket.
experiments.population: Orders with three or more line items and active production status.
experiments.primary_metric: Repeat status-check tickets within seven days
experiments.guardrail: No drop in order status page comprehension survey.
experiments.ship_decision: Ship if repeat tickets fall by 10 percent with no comprehension decline.

experiments.name: Exception ownership routing
experiments.hypothesis: Routing exceptions to an accountable owner with buyer-safe update copy will shorten resolution time.
experiments.population: Support tickets tagged delay, quality question, supplier handoff, or shipment exception.
experiments.guardrail: No increase in agent handle time beyond 8 percent.
experiments.ship_decision: Ship if resolution time improves by 15 percent and handle time remains within guardrail.

workflows.system_action: Create delay-alert candidate and request supplier reason code
workflows.buyer_output: Revised ETA, reason, confidence, and next update window

workflows.trigger: No supplier update for 72 hours during production
workflows.system_action: Flag stale milestone and route to supplier manager
workflows.buyer_output: Freshness indicator and next expected update

workflows.trigger: Support ticket repeats within seven days
workflows.system_action: Attach order timeline, prior messages, and recommended owner
workflows.buyer_output: Single case status with accountable next action

workflows.trigger: Shipment scan mismatch or partial shipment
workflows.system_action: Group carrier events by line item and document availability

researchNotes.insight: The buyer need is confidence that custom work is progressing, not only a status label.
researchNotes.evidence_type: Role requirement and public product support pattern

researchNotes.insight: The hardest moments occur at handoffs between buyer, supplier, operations, logistics, and support.
A PM needs support reasons, SLA risk, and milestone freshness tied to order journey moments.
